title: Handler Tests, Live-Protocol Tests, Fuzzing, Race Detection, and Contracts · Runtime Contract: Race Leak Load Test
summary: Tests call handler functions directly, inspect ResponseRecorder fields incorrectly, omit middleware and cancellation, never use TLS or concurrency, and assert only one status code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo race leak load test increment in layered deterministic server test system. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims.
Fuzz bounded request targets, headers, JSON, cursors, signatures, and policy inputs with invariants, seed corpus, reproducibility, and no live authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Race Leak Load Test · Theory Race Leak Load Test
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 1166 in a internal administrative API crossing two trust zones. Build deterministic Go evidence to gate release on handler, fuzz, race, and goroutine-lifecycle evidence; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Run race detection, concurrency schedules, leak checks, load profiles, resource limits, and failure injection while separating correctness from capacity claims.
